CoveredScenarios.Description: Session contains valid user; user has non-zero account balance; expect successful response with accounts and correct balance.
Session contains valid user; user has no accounts; expect response with empty accounts list and zero total balance.
Session does not contain user attribute; expect NullPointerException.
User ID parsing fails with non-numeric user ID; expect NumberFormatException.
Account repository returns null for total balance; expect successful response with accounts and null balance.
Account repository throws exception when fetching user accounts; expect RuntimeException.
Session contains valid user; user has a single account; expect response with single account and correct balance.
Account repository returns null for user account list; expect successful response with null userAccounts.
Session contains valid user; user ID is empty string; expect NumberFormatException.
Response map is correctly structured when user has accounts and balance; expect map contains userAccounts and totalBalance keys with proper values.

UncoveredScenarios.Description: Session contains user attribute, but user object is null; expect handling of null user without exceptions.
Session contains user with user_id = null; expect handling or exception.
Account repository throws exception when fetching total balance; expect error handling.
Account repository returns empty (not null) for user account list combined with null balance.
User ID is extremely large or small integer value, testing Integer boundaries.
Session attribute contains user, but user_id contains only whitespace; expect exception or error handling.
Session contains valid user; accountRepository returns accounts but no balance; edge case with missing balance field.
Concurrent request scenario where session changes during execution; verify method stability.
Session contains valid user; all fields are valid; method called multiple times to check idempotency.

CoveredScenarios.Description: User is present in session with multiple payment history records; response contains all records with status 200
User is present in session with no payment history records; response contains an empty list with status 200
User is absent in HTTP session; NullPointerException is thrown
User has a null or non-numeric user_id; NumberFormatException is thrown
Payment history repository returns null; response 'payment_history' key exists with null value and status 200
Concurrent users call getPaymentHistory; each receives correct payment history with respective user IDs
Response format contains only the 'payment_history' key
Payment history objects in response match the results returned from the repository
Session contains extra attributes alongside 'user'; payment history retrieval is unaffected

UncoveredScenarios.Description: User is present in session with a very large number of payment history records (testing scalability and performance of response)
Session is present but 'user' attribute is of incorrect type (not a User object)
User is present with a user_id corresponding to a non-existent user in the repository; repository returns an empty list
Corrupted PaymentHistory objects are returned from the repository (e.g., missing mandatory fields)
Session is expired or invalidated before accessing getPaymentHistory
Repository throws a runtime exception (e.g., database connection failure) when fetching records
Verifying that response headers (e.g., content type) are correctly set for all responses

CoveredScenarios.Description: Returns transaction history for authenticated user with existing transactions
Returns empty transaction history for user with no transactions
Throws NullPointerException when session has no 'user' attribute
Throws NumberFormatException when userId in user attribute is non-numeric
Returns response with null 'transaction_history' when repository returns null
Returns transaction history list containing null elements
Response contains exactly one map key: 'transaction_history'
Handles large transaction history list (e.g., 10,000 elements)
Throws ClassCastException when session 'user' attribute is wrong type
Throws NullPointerException when session object is null

UncoveredScenarios.Description: Handles case when returned transaction history list contains only null elements
Validates correct HTTP response structure when transaction history is partially null or contains custom objects beyond TransactionHistory
Ensures response does not expose sensitive user information in transaction history
Behavior if repository throws an unexpected runtime exception
Multiple concurrent requests for the same user and their impact on returned history
Correct typing and serialization of the response for different client accept headers (content negotiation)

CoveredScenarios.Description: Returns transaction history successfully when provided a valid account_id
Throws NullPointerException when account_id is missing in request map
Throws NumberFormatException when account_id is invalid format
Returns empty transaction history when there is no history for the given account
Handles large transaction history list for a valid account
Handles negative account_id and returns empty transaction history
Handles zero as account_id and returns empty transaction history
Ignores irrelevant keys in request map when valid account_id is provided
Handles null HttpSession gracefully with valid account_id
Handles repository returning null for the transaction history
Throws NumberFormatException for empty string as account_id
Throws RuntimeException when repository throws an exception
Verifies response structure for valid and invalid scenarios

UncoveredScenarios.Description: Handles non-integer numeric formats for account_id, such as floating-point numbers
Returns appropriate response when request map itself is null
Validates permission or session-based access (authorization/authentication of session)
Handles account_id values exceeding Integer.MAX_VALUE or less than Integer.MIN_VALUE
Validates response when the transaction history contains null items
Handles duplicate keys for account_id in request map (if possible based on input structure)
Validates situation when transactHistoryRepository is null
Validates if multiple concurrent calls behave correctly (thread safety)
